intent: Propose one falsifiable question and exact benchmarks that distinguish certificate stock, trade-triggered depletion, mechanical contemporaneous certification, and endogenous inspection, without solving or confirming the frame.

entity.title: Can cheaper certification induce a reversal through certificate depletion?
entity.summary: A proposed branch-specific test of whether lowering certificate cost can create a strict interior reversal in buyer search, service success, or buyer surplus through the stock and depletion of certificates.

payload.phenomenon: Lowering the cost of a truthful certificate changes both current issuance and the stationary public stock of unconsumed certificates; buyer choices then determine which certificates are depleted and which current capacities remain in the uncertified pool. This feedback may alter the return to inspecting an uncertified seller without imposing any directional claim.
payload.object_to_explain: For a fixed primitive vector and a connected branch of symmetric stationary Markov perfect equilibria, determine whether there are costs 0 <= k_L < k_M < k_H <= m and an outcome Y in {Q, A, U} such that (Y(k_M)-Y(k_L))*(Y(k_H)-Y(k_M)) < 0, where Q is the stationary probability that an arriving buyer inspects, A is the stationary probability of successful service per arriving buyer (exit counts as zero), and U is expected buyer service value minus inspection cost. Diagnose any such strict reversal with the benchmark set attached to this question.
payload.unresolved_delta: It is unresolved whether any admissible primitive vector and connected equilibrium branch produces a strict interior reversal, and, if one does, whether it is absent when intertemporal certificate stock or trade-triggered depletion is removed and whether it survives when the buyer's state-contingent inspection rule is fixed. No sign is presupposed for Q, A, or U.
payload.importance: The answer determines whether a policy comparison based only on the direct cost of certification or on contemporaneous certification take-up omits an information-inventory effect: certificates are public assets whose survival changes the composition of the residual uncertified pool and therefore the option value of costly inspection. The comparison matters for evaluating certification subsidies and information design, but this proposal states no welfare or policy direction.
payload.kill_condition: Kill the proposed consumable-stock explanation if an exhaustive characterization establishes that no admissible primitive vector, connected symmetric stationary equilibrium branch, and ordered cost triple satisfies the strict-reversal inequality together with the channel tests in the attached benchmarks; in particular, a reversal that occurs on every matched flow-only branch, or is unchanged when trade-triggered depletion is replaced by the matched exogenous-expiry law, does not qualify as evidence for this channel.
payload.proposed_scope: Two ex ante identical long-lived sellers face one short-lived buyer per period. Each seller has a binary public certificate stock s_i in {0,1}. At the start of a period, a seller with s_i=0 privately draws theta_i in {H,L}, independently across sellers and periods, with Pr(theta_i=H)=p in (0,1); an outstanding certificate is a reserved high-capacity service unit, so a seller with s_i=1 does not draw a new usable unit until that certificate is depleted. Before the buyer acts, an uncertified high-capacity seller may pay k to create one truthful certificate; low-capacity sellers cannot certify and certificate stocks cannot exceed one. The buyer observes the post-issuance public stocks, may pay c in (0,1) to inspect the current theta of exactly one uncertified seller, may buy without inspection, or may exit, and after any inspection chooses a seller or exits. Certified service succeeds with probability one and consumes that seller's certificate; uncertified service succeeds exactly when theta_i=H. Untraded certificates carry to the next period, while untraded uncertified capacity expires. A successful service gives the buyer one and gives the chosen seller a fixed margin m>0; the buyer pays only c when inspecting, and sellers discount by delta in (0,1). Uniform randomization breaks payoff ties. Study symmetric stationary Markov perfect equilibria, including mixed strategies. For multiplicity, evaluate each connected equilibrium branch separately over k; do not splice outcomes from different branches. Define Q(k), A(k), and U(k) under the branch's stationary distribution and use the strict-reversal product stated in object_to_explain.
prohibited_claims: No direction of the comparative static is claimed.
No existence, uniqueness, or stability result is claimed.
No novelty or literature-coverage claim is made.
No proof, validation, welfare ranking, or policy recommendation is asserted.
No benchmark is represented as an implemented or solved model.

entity.title: Benchmarks for certificate stock, depletion, and inspection
entity.summary: Three proposed counterfactuals isolate contemporaneous certification, trade-linked certificate depletion, and endogenous inspection while preserving the same outcome and reversal statistics.

benchmarks.label: Flow-only mechanical certification
exact_primitives: Set every seller's certificate stock to zero at the start of every period; no certificate carries across periods.

An uncertified high-capacity seller may still pay the same k before the buyer arrives, and the resulting truthful certificate guarantees service in that period only and expires after the buyer stage whether traded or not.
Keep p, c, m, delta, capacity draws, the buyer action set, tie-breaking, and seller and buyer optimization unchanged, and recompute a symmetric stationary equilibrium at each k.
timing: Reset certificate stocks to zero, draw current capacities, allow current high sellers to certify, let the buyer inspect or choose, realize service, and expire all current certificates before the next period.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Connected branches of symmetric stationary Markov perfect equilibria under the same branch-by-branch rule used for the full model.
benchmarks.prediction: No sign is imposed. Apply the same strict-reversal product to Q, A, and U. A reversal present here is compatible with contemporaneous cheaper certification and endogenous inspection without an intertemporal certificate-stock channel.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether the full-model reversal set strictly exceeds the flow-only reversal set at the same primitives and cost triple.

benchmarks.label: Matched exogenous certificate expiry
exact_primitives: Retain a binary certificate stock and the full model's issuance decision, but remove automatic consumption after a certified trade.

After the buyer stage, each outstanding certificate expires independently with a constant hazard h_0 that does not depend on trade, inspection, seller identity, capacity, or buyer choice; otherwise it carries to the next period and continues to guarantee certified service.

For each full-model branch and primitive vector, set h_0 equal to that branch's stationary probability at k_M that an outstanding certificate is consumed during a period, conditional on being outstanding at the buyer stage; hold this calibrated h_0 fixed at k_L, k_M, and k_H.
Keep all remaining primitives and endogenous seller issuance and buyer inspection choices unchanged and recompute equilibrium at each k.
timing: Carry outstanding certificates into the period, allow issuance by currently uncertified high sellers, let the buyer act and service occur without trade-triggered depletion, then apply the independent expiry draw h_0.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Connected branches of symmetric stationary Markov perfect equilibria, with h_0 calibrated from the paired full-model branch at k_M and then fixed across the cost triple.
benchmarks.prediction: No sign is imposed. Compare the same reversal products with the full model. A difference after matching the reference depletion hazard isolates the dependence of depletion on buyer trade rather than the mean persistence of a certificate stock.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether any full-model reversal disappears when the same reference depletion rate is made independent of buyer choices.

benchmarks.label: Fixed state-contingent inspection rule
exact_primitives: Keep the full consumable-certificate stock, issuance, carryover, and trade-triggered depletion laws.

For each full-model branch, record at k_M the buyer's inspection probability q_0(s_1,s_2) and, when inspection occurs, the lottery over eligible uncertified sellers for every public post-issuance stock state.

At k_L, k_M, and k_H impose q_0 and the recorded inspection-target lottery at each public state; allow the buyer's purchase or exit choice conditional on the imposed inspection outcome and allow seller issuance choices to reoptimize at each k.
Compute aggregate Q under the endogenous stationary distribution, so Q may change through public-state composition even though the state-contingent inspection rule is fixed.
timing: Use the full model's timing, replacing only the buyer's inspection decision and inspection-target selection by the k_M state-contingent rule before the buyer's conditional purchase or exit choice.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Symmetric stationary seller equilibria and buyer conditional best responses subject to the imposed branch-specific inspection rule q_0.
benchmarks.prediction: No sign is imposed. Compare reversal products with the full model. Persistence under this benchmark is compatible with a composition effect operating through the stationary distribution; disappearance indicates that reoptimization of the state-contingent inspection margin is required.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether a full-model reversal survives after fixing the buyer's state-contingent inspection rule while retaining endogenous certificate issuance and depletion.

payload.exact_question_delta: The flow-only benchmark retains mechanical current certification and endogenous inspection while deleting intertemporal certificate stock; the matched-expiry benchmark retains a stock and matches its reference depletion rate while deleting the dependence of depletion on buyer trade; the fixed-inspection benchmark retains the full stock and depletion law while deleting reoptimization of the state-contingent inspection rule. At identical primitives and 0 <= k_L < k_M < k_H <= m, compare (Y(k_M)-Y(k_L))*(Y(k_H)-Y(k_M)) for each Y in {Q,A,U} branch by branch. A proposed consumable-stock case requires a strict negative product in the full model that is not reproduced by the flow-only benchmark; the matched-expiry and fixed-inspection comparisons then distinguish trade-linked depletion from mean persistence and endogenous inspection response.

outcome.rationale: Proposes exactly one falsifiable research question and one benchmark set with three exact channel deltas for later human G1 consideration; it does not solve, validate, or confirm the frame.
